For Edge Integration Cell, as the standard UI is not available there, you might need to perform some actions by using the Partner Directory API of Edge Integration Cell. Still, the tool simplifies your interactions with the Partner Directory a lot, especially on Edge Integration Cell, so make sure to give it a try!

 

3       Use Partner Directory on Edge Integration Cell to run the Pipeline for Cloud Integration

3.1     Copy and deploy generic Integration Flows

To setup the Pipeline on Edge Integration Cell, start by copying and deploying the generic Integration Flows. If you have already set up the Pipeline in the same tenant, the recommendation is to copy the standard packages again to your tenant, e.g. with prefix “EIC” for Edge Integration Cell, in order to distinguish this package from the package used in the cloud. With that, you are independent in configuring the standard Integration Flows, like for custom error handling or for custom preprocessing.

As minimum, you need the following Integration Flows:

  • Pipeline v2 Generic Step02 – Integrated Messaging Runtime Async from package “Cloud Integration Pipeline – Generic Pipelines”
  • Pipeline v2 Generic Step06 – Outbound Processing from package “Cloud Integration Pipeline – Generic Outbound”

When deploying, make sure to select your Edge Integration Cell as runtime profile.

 

3.2     Create and deploy scenario-specific Integration Flows

As usual, you need Integration Flows step01 for scenario-specific inbound and step07 for scenario-specific outbound, which must be created and deployed on Edge Integration Cell. Depending on your use case, you might want to set up generic IDoc or XI inbound for step01 or you might find a helpful template as a starting point.

 

3.3     Setup Partner Directory entries on Edge Integration Cell

3.3.1      Setup Partner Directory entries on Edge Integration Cell from scratch

As the accelerator tool is now running, you can start using it to set up your Partner Directory entries on Edge Integration Cell, which you need to run the Pipeline.

To do that, add a new alternative partner, save the entry, and maintain receiver and interface determination. Optionally, you can maintain string parameters, landscape stages, and more, to configure your scenario.

To try the Pipeline out with some samples, you can follow this guide using sample scenarios.

 

3.3.2      Alternative: Migrate from Pipeline without Partner Directory on Edge Integration Cell to Pipeline with Partner Directory

In case you have been running the Pipeline without the Partner Directory on Edge Integration Cell before, you might want to migrate these scenarios, as this brings you much more flexibility and does not require additional Integration Flows for custom receiver and interface determinations.

For that, take the headers configured in step01 and set them up as Partner Directory entries, similar to the approach above when setting the entries up from scratch. For receiver and interface determination, preferably choose either a combined XSLT for both receiver and interface determination, or go for a point-to-point scenario if no content-based routing is required.

 

3.3.3      Alternative: Replicate Partner Directory entries from cloud to Edge Integration Cell

In case you want to run some of your cloud scenarios now on Edge Integration Cell, you can nicely replicate the respective Partner Directory entries from your cloud Partner Directory to your Partner Directory on Edge Integration Cell. For that, simply confirm, and let the magic happen. Once done, the view will end up on the new Partner Directory entries on Edge Integration Cell.

 

3.3.4      Alternative: Migration tool for Pipeline on Edge Integration Cell

When coming from an SAP Process Orchestration / SAP Process Integration system, you can use the Pipeline to migrate to SAP Integration Suite. The migration tool supports the Pipeline and can also be used for the Pipeline on Edge Integration Cell! To do this, use the migration tool as usual to generate the artifacts and the Partner Directory entries. Both are intended to be used with the cloud runtime but can be used on Edge Integration Cell as well. For that, select your Edge Integration Cell as runtime profile when deploying the generated Integration Flows for step01 and step07. For the Partner Directory entries, you can use the replication feature of the accelerator tool as explained above.

 

3.4     Run the Pipeline by sending a message to Edge Integration Cell

To see the Pipeline running on Edge Integration Cell, send a message to trigger step01 of the Pipeline on Edge Integration Cell. In your SAP Integration Suite tenant under Monitor, select your Edge Integration Cell from the runtime dropdown. You can filter by correlation ID to get an overview of all Integration Flows which have been called to run your scenario.
